historical_context.location: Deck of a wooden whaling ship, coastal New England
historical_context.description: A rare group portrait showing the diverse origins of a single whaling crew.

subjects.traits: 21yo, light skin, striking blue eyes, messy black hair, rough wool peacoat
21yo, light skin, brown hair, black eyes, officer cap, holding brass sextant
20yo, dark skin, dark eyes, dark hair, linen shirt, holding a toggle-iron harpoon

cinematography.camera: Large format bellows camera, Petzval lens
cinematography.lighting: Natural daylight, soft shadows, realistic skin oil reflections
cinematography.framing: Three-quarter length group portrait, standing side-by-side

master_prompt: A genuine, museum-quality 1885 Cabinet Card photograph of three whaling crew members standing together on a ship's deck. In the center is a 21-year-old 3rd Mate from Rochester, neatly groomed with brown hair and sharp black eyes, wearing a captain's cap and holding a tarnished brass sextant. To his left stands a 21-year-old Seaman from New Bedford, characterized by striking blue eyes and messy black hair under a rough wool cap. To the right is a 20-year-old Portuguese Boatsteerer from the Azores, his dark skin deeply tanned and glistening, holding an iron harpoon. The image is an authentic albumen print with a warm sepia-yellow tone and subtle silver mirroring in the dark areas. At the bottom of the thick cardboard margin, there is realistic, slightly faded handwritten ink cursive text that reads: 'Crew of the Bark Wanderer - 1885'. The photo exhibits natural age-related foxing, micro-cracks, and uneven development. No digital artifacts, completely unposed and gritty.
negative_prompt: ai-generated, digital art, 3d, rendering, smooth skin, symmetrical faces, modern handwriting, typeface, clean clothes, studio lighting, plastic, vibrant colors, sharpened, watermark
